# Blueprint v0.2.3 — first release with installable artifacts

v0.2.0, v0.2.1, and v0.2.2 all attempted to ship the same code; all
three releases failed in CI before producing artifacts. v0.2.3 is the
first build where every platform target succeeded end-to-end.

## What was wrong with v0.2.2

After `KERNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN` was added and the private `blueprint-cli`
module fetch finally succeeded, three independent platform bugs were
exposed:

- **Linux.** Ubuntu 24 (now the default `ubuntu-latest`) dropped
  `libwebkit2gtk-4.0-dev`. The workflow installs `-4.1-dev`, but Wails
  defaults to the 4.0 bindings and `pkg-config` couldn't find them.
  Fixed by passing `-tags webkit2_41` to the Linux build.
- **macOS.** The post-build `ditto` step looked for `blueprint.app`,
  but Wails packages the bundle using `wails.json`'s `name` field
  (`blueprint-app`). Fixed by referencing `blueprint-app.app`.
- **Windows.** Same root cause as macOS — the NSIS installer is named
  `blueprint-app-amd64-installer.exe`, not `blueprint-amd64-...`.
  Fixed by updating the artifact glob.

## Code surface

Identical to v0.2.2 — the v0.2.3 commit only touches
`.github/workflows/release.yml`. The Ed25519 production public key
that landed in v0.2.2 ships unchanged in v0.2.3.

For everything new since v0.1.x, see [`0.2.0.md`](./0.2.0.md): SSH-
tunneled multi-host control plane, push-install of `blueprint-svc`,
remote-aware Models / Overview / Inference tabs, streamed chat over
SSH, live `/v1/snapshot` for remote system tiles, NSIS installer
bundling the supervisor.

## Install

**Windows.** Download `blueprint-windows-amd64-installer.exe`, run
it. Admin elevation required to register the Windows Service.

**Linux.** Download `blueprint-svc-linux` + `install-linux.sh`.
`sudo bash install-linux.sh` registers the systemd unit. Desktop UI:
download `blueprint-linux-amd64`, run.

**macOS.** Download `blueprint-macos-universal.app.zip`, extract,
open.

## Known limitations

- Windows installer is unsigned; SmartScreen will warn on first run.
  Signing certificate is the v0.2.4 target.
- macOS .app is self-signed (developer build); first run requires
  right-click → Open. Notarization unlocks once Apple Developer ID
  secrets are added to the workflow.
- Engine-plan compilation for TensorRT-LLM is not wrapped; Blueprint
  loads pre-built `.engine` plans produced externally with
  `trtllm-build`.
